name: Seimeiras de Queixoiro Loop Trail
name_original: Cascadas de Queixoiro – circular desde Queixoiro por Ermita de Santa Bárbara de Silvela
description: Hike a moderate 9.2-mile circular route from Queixoiro to the Queixoiro Waterfalls and the Hermitage of Santa Bárbara de Silvela.
region_intro: Hiking around A Fonsagrada offers diverse trails through a landscape characterized by rolling hills, dense woodlands, and river valleys. The region features numerous waterfalls and historical sites, providing varied scenery for outdoor exploration. Trails often follow ancient paths, connecting small villages and offering views of the Galician countryside. Best hiking trails around A Fonsagrada The most popular hiking route is Seimeiras de Queixoiro Loop Trail, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 20 minutes to complete, leading through areas known for their natural waterfalls. Another top favourite among local hikers is Desfiladero de Bustelín (PR-AS 27) — circular desde Riodeporcos, a difficult 9.7 miles (15.6 km) path. This route navigates a scenic gorge, offering challenging terrain and impressive views. Local hikers also love the Queixoiro Waterfalls loop from Queixoiro, a 2.3 miles (3.8 km) trail leading through lush forest to a series of waterfalls, often completed in about 1 hour 7 minutes. Hiking around A Fonsagrada is defined by its verdant river valleys, historic sites, and cascading waterfalls. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy hikes to more challenging ascents. The routes in A Fonsagrada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore A Fonsagrada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around A Fonsagrada?
A: There are over 40 hiking trails in the A Fonsagrada region, catering to various skill levels. This includes 10 easy routes, 24 moderate trails, and 6 more challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking options in A Fonsagrada?
A: Yes, A Fonsagrada offers 10 easy hiking routes suitable for families or those looking for a relaxed walk. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14985587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queixoiro Waterfalls loop from Queixoiro&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.3 miles (3.8 km) long and leads through lush forest to a series of waterfalls.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in A Fonsagrada?
A: Hike durations vary significantly. Easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14985587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queixoiro Waterfalls loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in just over an hour. Moderate routes, such as the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979347508/seimeiras-de-queixoiro-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeiras de Queixoiro Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 4 hours 20 minutes. More difficult trails can extend to 5-8 hours or more.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in A Fonsagrada?
A: Yes, many trails in A Fonsagrada are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979347508/seimeiras-de-queixoiro-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeiras de Queixoiro Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240938491/desfiladero-de-bustelin-pr-as-27-circular-desde-riodeporcos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de Bustelín (PR-AS 27) — circular desde Riodeporcos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see on hikes in A Fonsagrada?
A: The region is known for its verdant river valleys, dense woodlands, and numerous waterfalls. You can explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Salgueira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Murias Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5416282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Miño River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there historical sites or landmarks to explore near the trails?
A: Absolutely. A Fonsagrada's trails often follow ancient paths connecting small villages and historical sites. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4527080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montouto Hospital and Dolmen&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornos de Vilaoudriz&lt;/a&gt;, and various religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4530231" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Carmen de Vilabade&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in A Fonsagrada?
A: The best time to hike in A Fonsagrada is typically during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant, with blooming flora in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summer can also be pleasant, though some days might be warmer.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in A Fonsagrada?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in A Fonsagrada. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife, and to follow local regulations regarding pet access.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about A Fonsagrada?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 140 reviews. Reviewers often praise the region's diverse scenery, including its natural waterfalls and lush river valleys, as well as the well-maintained network of trails that offer options for various ability levels.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, A Fonsagrada offers 6 difficult trails for experienced hikers. One such challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240938491/desfiladero-de-bustelin-pr-as-27-circular-desde-riodeporcos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de Bustelín (PR-AS 27) — circular desde Riodeporcos&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.7-mile (15.6 km) path that navigates a scenic gorge, offering demanding terrain and impressive views.

Q: What should I pack for a hike in A Fonsagrada?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for changing weather, it's recommended to pack layers of clothing, waterproof gear, sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water and snacks, a map or GPS device, and sun protection. For longer hikes, consider a first-aid kit and a power bank for your phone.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads in A Fonsagrada?
A: While A Fonsagrada is a rural area, some villages and towns serving as trailheads may have limited bus services. However, for maximum flexibility and access to more remote starting points, traveling by car is generally recommended. Always check local transport schedules in advance.

Q: Are there cafes or places to eat near the hiking trails?
A: Many trails in A Fonsagrada connect small villages where you can often find local cafes, bars, or restaurants offering traditional Galician cuisine. It's a good idea to plan your route to pass through these villages for a refreshment stop or to carry your own provisions, especially on longer or more remote trails.
tips: In the middle of the Queixoiro forest, next to the Rodil river, there are two magical waterfalls. The way to the pools is covered by beautiful wooden bridges and paths surrounded by lush vegetation and large chestnut trees.

This hermitage is located in a meadow with views. There are picnic areas to sit and rest. On the door of the chapel there is an inscription that says "Santa Bárbara blessed, that in heaven you are written, keep bread and keep wine for all good pilgrims".

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The circular trail starts from Queixoiro. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's generally advisable to look for designated parking areas near the village or the trailhead. As a popular hiking area, local amenities often include parking for visitors.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail features mostly accessible paths, but be prepared for some sections, especially near the Queixoiro Waterfalls, that involve descending stairs and potentially slippery stones. You'll hike through deep valleys with a rich diversity of flora, including ancient chestnut trees, pines, oaks, and ash trees, creating a 'magical forest' atmosphere.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlights are the two beautiful and tall Queixoiro Waterfalls (locally known as 'seimeiras') on the Tronceda River, with stairs and walkways providing access to viewpoints. Another significant point is the picturesque Hermitage of Santa Bárbara de Silvela, which offers pleasant views and picnic spots.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed in various seasons, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant conditions with lush greenery and comfortable temperatures. Visiting after rainfall can enhance the waterfalls' flow, making them even more spectacular.

faqs.question: Is the Cascadas de Queixoiro circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, specific regulations regarding dogs are not detailed. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other hikers, and to clean up after them. Always check local signage for any restrictions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned for hiking this trail. It is generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: The full circular route is rated as 'moderate' and requires good physical condition due to its length and elevation changes. While some shorter sections around the waterfalls might be easier, the entire 14.8 km loop might be challenging for younger children or those not accustomed to longer hikes. Hikers should be sure-footed.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with any other well-known hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Etapa 7: Grandas de Salime - A Fonsagrada and the Senda das Seimeiras de Queixoiro.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varying terrain, it's advisable to bring sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, and appropriate clothing for the weather. Trekking poles can be helpful for stability on slippery sections or stairs. Don't forget a camera for the waterfalls!

faqs.question: Are there facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Queixoiro in the municipality of A Fonsagrada. While specific facilities directly at the trailhead are not detailed, A Fonsagrada is a town where you can typically find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. The Hermitage of Santa Bárbara de Silvela also offers picnic areas for a rest stop.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this hike?
faqs.answer: This specific circular route is rated as 'moderate'. It covers nearly 15 kilometers with an elevation gain of over 400 meters, requiring good physical condition and sure-footedness, especially on sections with stairs and potentially slippery stones near the waterfalls.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Santa Bárbara de Silvela
